content.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   I am acting autonomously as **Ryan Delgado, Audit Senior at Brookfield CPAs & Advisors** (no further user input; current date fixed at **June 12, 2026** — must ignore the system reminder's June 15 date). Ryan suspects that reconciliation exception write-ups repeatedly promise the same corrective action when closed, but the fixes are never actually made, so the same breakdowns keep getting re-logged. He wants this evidenced for **Julia** (cc **Devon**) ahead of a control review. Explicit deliverables:
   - **(A) Closed history analysis:** Identify which breakdowns recur vs. true one-offs; for each recurring one, determine whether the documented fix was ever implemented or the same problem resurfaced later. Quantify real scope — how many periods, which entities, where it keeps landing.
   - **(B) Open items:** For EVERY reconciliation still carrying an exception, leave a **review note** capturing the REAL disposition (booked, or accepted as a timing difference; which period; who made it / who it's waiting on — "that decision is not on the face of the record" and "the BlackLine status is the last thing I would trust"), and whether it's a recurring breakdown or genuine one-off. Single out any flagged **systemic but still unresolved**.
   - **(C) Overdue, never-finished reconciliations:** Leave a **review note** on each capturing how long it's been open and what is unreconciled; call out accounts that go overdue period after period.
   - **(D)** Write it up for the **workpapers** (Records Vault).
   - **(E)** Raise the recurring breakdown as a **single tracked Linear issue** (NOT one per cause) with a real owner.
   - **(F)** Email findings to **Julia, cc Devon** ahead of the review.
   - **(G)** Post a short summary in the **audit Slack channel**.
   Guidance: go to primary/source data (Oracle GL, SAP) for precision; don't trust secondhand status; make parallel independent tool calls.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - SOX month-end close, BlackLine reconciliation lifecycle (open → in_progress → submitted → approved → certified → archived; plus overdue), exception lifecycle (logged → investigating → awaiting_approval → closed).
   - Four-eyes / three-role SOX controls (preparer/reviewer/certifier distinct).
   - Multi-entity GL: entities are **brookfield**, **northstar_legal**, **acme_cloud**; period IDs like `brookfield_FP-2026-05`.
   - Recurring root-cause classification; corrective JE tracking via `corrective_journal_entry_id`; "BD3 cutover" / "BD3 lock" close milestones.

content.text: GL source confirms the thesis conclusively: brookfield account **227000 has a flat balance of $2,203,570.05 across every period from 2025-07 onward, with $0 debits and $0 credits in FP-2025-09** — no corrective JE was ever booked, despite closed exception exc_143730e61ce742 claiming "Corrective JE posted; variance cleared." The −595.28 variance simply vanished from the supporting side in the next period and the recon (BL-CBBC1C9D68DD) was abandoned in overdue status. The 227000 series also shows the recurrence: overdue again at FP-2026-02 (BL-1D75DEF62AFE). I have source-verified evidence. Let me record progress and pull the 10 open-exception reconciliations to cite exact figures.

content.text:
Major finding: two of the three systemic-but-unresolved open exceptions sit on reconciliations BlackLine shows as **certified/archived** — signed off as clean while the exception remains open and unbooked:
- **BL-BF341D66FE59** (ns 120000, FP-2025-09): **certified** 2025-10-10, yet exc_3e121025060b4b is still *awaiting_approval* — carries −1,103.75 ("adjusting entry pending Manager approval") + −441.50 residual.
- **BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2** (acme 101000 Cash, FP-2025-11): **archived** 2026-01-05, yet exc_abd8e711f5f347 still *awaiting_approval* — +1,014.33 + 405.73 residual.

This is precisely why BlackLine status can't be trusted. Let me fetch the remaining 7 open-exception recons to capture each recon's true state before writing notes.

content.text: I now have the true state of all 10 open-exception reconciliations. Three sit on recons certified/archived over open exceptions; the rest are open/in_progress with unposted corrections. Writing the 10 review notes now (Part B), each capturing the real disposition, recurring-vs-one-off, and systemic-unresolved flags.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_06b89e3937b04a (investigating; assigned anaya.wallace). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 1,763,703.31 vs supporting 1,763,875.93; the -172.62 sits on the supporting side as a manual reclassification that has NOT been posted to oracle_gl (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). This is not yet an evidenced timing difference — it is an unposted reclass tracked only in the exception log. Pattern: post-BD3 subledger item not picked up by the GL feed = the recurring close breakdown, not an isolated one-off. Do not approve until the reclass is posted, or the item is evidenced as a genuine timing difference with the clearing period identified.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d (investigating, HIGH urgency; assigned anaya.wallace). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 2,139,388.27 exceeds supporting 2,134,997.65 by +4,390.62 — likely a missed accrual against an open vendor invoice. The proposed fix is a manual corrective JE in oracle_gl referencing the exception_id, but corrective_journal_entry_id=null — nothing has been posted. Logged cause is a partial-success subledger feed retry (remainder pending): the recurring SAP->Oracle feed / BD3 breakdown, not a one-off. Do not approve until the accrual JE is posted and the recon re-run.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). CONTROL CONCERN: this recon is marked CERTIFIED (certified 2025-10-10 by steven.perry; approved by jones.harrison) yet its exception exc_3e121025060b4b is STILL OPEN (awaiting_approval; flagged SYSTEMIC; waiting on approver edith.banda). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. The -1,103.75 described as "adjusting entry pending Manager approval; will clear in resubmit" was never posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null), and a further -441.50 "residual unexplained" was deferred to the next-period close package. Certified status overstates completion — the variance was signed off, not resolved. SYSTEMIC AND UNRESOLVED. Account 120000 Client Cost Advances also went overdue at FP-2025-07 (BL-43515365C411): recurring breakdown, not a one-off.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_4d5d3582698946 (investigating, HIGH urgency; assigned emily.adekole). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 195,774.22 vs supporting 201,843.13; the -6,068.91 is flagged as a duplicate-posting suspect / partial-success feed batch. Proposed manual corrective JE not posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). RECURRING breakdown: account 131000 Prepaid Software carried the same duplicate-entry exception at FP-2025-10 (exc_ae607d93ad364a), which was closed as "Corrective JE posted" with NO JE on file — same problem resurfaced this period. Do not approve until the duplicate is confirmed and reversed in oracle_gl.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_652c0931bb2546 (awaiting_approval, low urgency; waiting on approver daniel.jones). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. +9.61 timing difference (above the $5 threshold) attributed to an intercompany clearing JE posted to the wrong entity code; no correcting entry posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). Immaterial in isolation, but 260000 Partner Distributions Payable is a RECURRING account — overdue at brookfield FP-2026-03 (BL-6C4B634CDCB8) and a closed duplicate-entry exception on ns 260000 at FP-2025-11 (exc_4e5838d006f64c). Confirm the IC clearing routes to the correct entity rather than re-accepting the same drift period after period.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_a0f77f2a19104e (awaiting_approval, HIGH urgency; SLA breached 2026-05-26; waiting on approver ryan.delgado). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 67,744.03 vs supporting 61,415.17; the +6,328.86 is described as "adjusting entry pending Manager approval" — a duplicate-posting suspect on AP. No corrective JE posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). RECURRING AP breakdown: account 210000 AP-External Vendors also overdue at ns FP-2025-12 (BL-91589384DD4C) and acme FP-2025-09 (BL-BE53BACC82D5). input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_aade06f6129e43 (state: LOGGED only — no root cause or proposed resolution recorded yet; assigned blue.evans). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked and not yet investigated. -28.59 from a subledger feed that dropped 29 rows; supporting reflects only the partial load. RECURRING on a CASH account: 102000 Cash-Payroll was overdue at brookfield FP-2025-10 (BL-B62A9864AADD), closed-but-unbooked at brookfield FP-2025-12 (exc_d8fc13aa2cc742), and overdue at ns FP-2025-12 (BL-154CAE8E937B) and ns FP-2026-02 (BL-165E8FCE2971). A cash account should not carry an unresolved feed drop — escalate the dropped-feed retry; do not accept as timing.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). CONTROL CONCERN: this recon is ARCHIVED into the PBC binder (certified by rakesh.ambani; archived 2026-01-05) yet its exception exc_abd8e711f5f347 is STILL OPEN (awaiting_approval; flagged SYSTEMIC; waiting on approver george.mcadam). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. The +1,014.33 attributed to an intercompany clearing JE posted to the wrong entity code, plus +405.73 "residual unexplained," were neither corrected (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). A CASH account was archived with an open, unresolved systemic exception. SYSTEMIC AND UNRESOLVED. Account 101000 Cash-Operating recurs across entities (ns FP-2026-05 open, brookfield FP-2025-12 closed-unbooked, ns FP-2025-10 overdue).

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_af7274fb658844 (investigating; flagged SYSTEMIC; assigned sean.williams). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 1,998,167.81 vs supporting 1,998,740.68; the -572.87 is logged as a timing difference / FX revaluation rates refreshed after the closing snapshot. No correcting entry posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null) and it has run past SLA. SYSTEMIC AND UNRESOLVED. 101000 Cash-Operating is a RECURRING breakdown across all three entities. Confirm whether this is a genuine FX timing difference (identify the clearing period) or an unposted correction before any approval.

input.author: ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: AUDIT REVIEW (R. Delgado, control-review prep, 2026-06-12). Open exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c (awaiting_approval, FX revaluation drift; waiting on approver james.randall). REAL DISPOSITION: NOT booked. GL 1,075,804.83 vs supporting 1,075,369.42; the +435.41 is attributed to three vendor invoices arriving after BD3 lock (caught in next-period feed). Plausible timing difference but NOT evidenced to a clearing period, and no entry posted (corrective_journal_entry_id=null). RECURRING: 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance went overdue at FP-2025-11 (BL-409A3095FF02) and FP-2026-03 (BL-55151C28DEC1) — the same prepaid never gets cleanly reconciled. Tie the late invoices to the next-period feed and document the clearing before accepting as timing.

content.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   I am acting autonomously as **Ryan Delgado, Audit Senior at Brookfield CPAs & Advisors** (no further user input; task-authoritative date is **June 12, 2026** — I must IGNORE the system reminder's June 15 date). Ryan suspects reconciliation exception write-ups repeatedly promise the same corrective action when closed, but the fixes are never actually booked, so the same breakdowns keep getting re-logged. He wants this evidenced for **Julia** (cc **Devon**) ahead of a control review. He is "documenting where things stand, not making fixes or clearing reminders." Explicit deliverables:
   - **(A) Closed history analysis:** Identify recurring breakdowns vs. true one-offs; for each recurring one, determine whether the documented fix was ever implemented or the same problem resurfaced. Quantify real scope — how many periods, which entities, where it keeps landing.
   - **(B) Open items:** For EVERY reconciliation still carrying an exception, leave a **review note** capturing the REAL disposition (booked, or accepted as timing difference; which period; who made it / who it's waiting on — "the BlackLine status is the last thing I would trust"), and whether it's a recurring breakdown or genuine one-off. Single out any flagged **systemic but still unresolved**.
   - **(C) Overdue, never-finished reconciliations:** Leave a **review note** on each capturing how long it's been open and what is unreconciled; call out accounts that go overdue period after period.
   - **(D)** Write it up for the **workpapers** (Records Vault).
   - **(E)** Raise the recurring breakdown as a **single tracked Linear issue** (NOT one per cause) with a real owner.
   - **(F)** Email findings to **Julia, cc Devon** ahead of the review.
   - **(G)** Post a short summary in the **audit Slack channel**.
   Guidance: go to primary/source data (Oracle GL, SAP) for precision; don't trust secondhand status; make parallel independent tool calls.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - SOX month-end close; BlackLine reconciliation lifecycle (open → in_progress → submitted → approved → certified → archived; plus overdue); exception lifecycle (logged → investigating → awaiting_approval → closed).
   - Four-eyes / three-role SOX controls (preparer/reviewer/certifier distinct).
   - Multi-entity GL: entities **brookfield**, **northstar_legal**, **acme_cloud**; period IDs like `brookfield_FP-2026-05`.
   - "BD3 cutover/lock" close milestone; SAP→Oracle subledger feed drops; corrective JE tracking via `corrective_journal_entry_id`.

content.content: {"result":"[{\"id\": \"linear_7d4b3f1127cd\", \"name\": \"Tax & Compliance Delivery\", \"key\": \"TCD\", \"description\": \"External client tax, entity compliance, and filing readiness team overseeing tax document chase lists, annual report catch-up, filing calendar exceptions, and audit/PBC support. The team reviews due-date risk tied to vendor support items like VEN-004-799133, follows period-specific adjustments including JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0004 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0002, and escalates unresolved approval items such as exc_abd8e711f5f347 and exc_cb0a9a94a3084c when they affect filing readiness or advisory delivery.\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-04-14T09:20:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-31T15:35:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"name\": \"Client Accounting Operations\", \"key\": \"CAO\", \"description\": \"Partner-led outsourced accounting team handling daily close blocker triage, client bank feed exceptions, cash posting follow-up, AP workflow reviews, and bookkeeping remediation for external clients. The team regularly works through reconciliation items like BL-18FFAE0E20D0 on 117000 Allowance for Doubtful Accounts, investigates open exceptions such as exc_af7274fb658844 and exc_4d5d3582698946, and coordinates invoice processing across items including VEN-019-583136 and VEN-012-211225 during monthly close and onboarding cycles.\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-03-03T08:45:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-20T17:10:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_acctops\", \"name\": \"Accounting Operations\", \"key\": \"ACCT\", \"description\": \"Accounting services delivery, review chains, and portfolio execution\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-11-10T17:55:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-11-10T17:55:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_tax\", \"name\": \"Tax\", \"key\": \"TAX\", \"description\": \"Corporate tax review, filings, and tax-accounting coordination\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-10-19T07:43:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-10-19T07:43:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_audit\", \"name\": \"Audit\", \"key\": \"AUD\", \"description\": \"Audit support, workpapers, and engagement review\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-02-24T17:31:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-02-24T17:31:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_comp\", \"name\": \"Compliance\", \"key\": \"COMP\", \"description\": \"AML, privacy, and policy oversight\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2021-04-20T19:10:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2021-04-20T19:10:00Z\"}]"}

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   I am acting autonomously as **Ryan Delgado, Audit Senior at Brookfield CPAs & Advisors** (no further user input; task-authoritative date is **June 12, 2026** — I must IGNORE system reminders showing June 15). Ryan suspects reconciliation exception write-ups repeatedly promise the same corrective action when closed, but the fixes are never actually booked, so the same breakdowns keep getting re-logged. He wants this evidenced for **Julia (cc Devon)** ahead of a control review. He is "documenting where things stand, not making fixes or clearing reminders." Explicit deliverables:
   - **(A) Closed history analysis:** recurring breakdowns vs. true one-offs; for each recurring one, determine whether the documented fix was ever implemented or the same problem resurfaced. Quantify real scope — how many periods, which entities, where it keeps landing.
   - **(B) Open items:** for EVERY reconciliation still carrying an exception, leave a **review note** capturing the REAL disposition (booked, or accepted timing difference; which period; who made it / who it's waiting on — "the BlackLine status is the last thing I would trust"), recurring vs one-off, and single out any flagged **systemic but still unresolved**.
   - **(C) Overdue, never-finished reconciliations:** leave a **review note** on each capturing how long it's been open and what is unreconciled; call out accounts that go overdue period after period.
   - **(D)** Write it up for the **workpapers** (Records Vault).
   - **(E)** Raise the recurring breakdown as a **single tracked Linear issue** (NOT one per cause) with a real owner.
   - **(F)** Email findings to **Julia, cc Devon** ahead of the review.
   - **(G)** Post a short summary in the **audit Slack channel**.
   Guidance: go to primary/source data (Oracle GL, SAP) for precision; don't trust secondhand status; make parallel independent tool calls.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - SOX month-end close; BlackLine reconciliation lifecycle (open → in_progress → submitted → approved → certified → archived; plus overdue); exception lifecycle (logged → investigating → awaiting_approval → closed).
   - Four-eyes / three-role SOX controls (preparer/reviewer/certifier distinct).
   - Multi-entity GL: entities **brookfield**, **northstar_legal**, **acme_cloud**; period IDs like `brookfield_FP-2026-05`.
   - "BD3 cutover/lock" close milestone; SAP→Oracle subledger feed drops; corrective JE tracking via `corrective_journal_entry_id` (null = never booked).
   - MCP servers: blackline, oracle_gl, sap_subledger, records_vault, linear, email, slack, contacts, messaging, calendar, reminder, airtable, peeragentsmcp.
   - Review note creation: `blackline_create_review_note(recon_id, author, body)` — author = `ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com`. Server auto-stamps created_at 2026-06-15 with SLA 2026-06-17 (system clock; cannot change).

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/d4cd6e96-a7df-45ae-877d-82d972e369ea/tool-results/mcp-mcp_01714022-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781484729420.txt** — overflowed brookfield_FP-2025-10 JE list; NOT needed (account 227000 zero-activity already proven via get_account_balance).
   - **/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/d4cd6e96-a7df-45ae-877d-82d972e369ea/tool-results/mcp-mcp_01714022-blackline_list_reconciliations-1781484402054.txt** — full unfiltered reconciliations list (683 total).
   - No source-code files; this is a data-investigation + actions task via MCP tools.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **oracle_gl_list_journal_entries (brookfield_FP-2025-10) exceeded max tokens** → auto-saved to file; did not need it (relied on `oracle_gl_get_account_balance` 227000 FP-2025-09 = $0 debits/$0 credits).
   - **Owner-lookup dead ends:** `contacts_search_contacts("Controller")`, `("Sandra Okonkwo")`, `("Sandra")` and `linear_list_users(query="controller")` all returned empty. Fix: "Sandra Okonkwo" is generic tool-docstring text, not in this universe. Resolved by pulling close-task review chains + full Linear user directory + closed-exception approvers to identify a real accountable owner.
   - **Recurring `<system-reminder>` interjections:** (a) June 15 currentDate — I continue using authoritative **June 12, 2026**; (b) repeated TodoWrite nudges — I maintain/update the 7-item list. Never mention reminders to the user.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Core thesis proven with source evidence (this session, from `blackline_list_exceptions(state=closed)`):** All **14** closed exceptions have `corrective_journal_entry_id=null` despite identical boilerplate `resolution_summary` "Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh" and identical `lessons_learned` "Update BD3 cutover checklist to include this feed; flag in next-period control test." All escalated=true, sox_implications=true, urgency n_a. **6 carry systemic_issue_flag=true:** exc_143730e61ce742 (227000), exc_151b0bee7e374e (110000), exc_3e229b67ef6147 (103000), exc_5221568d4e4047 (235000), exc_91530732a38046 (133000), exc_d8fc13aa2cc742 (102000). Approvers of never-booked closures: jones.harrison, james.randall (×4), hannah.grant (×2), ryan.delgado (×3 — me), alex.cahoon, daniel.jones, edith.banda, george.mcadam.
   - **Keystone (GL-verified earlier):** exc_143730e61ce742 (brookfield 227000, FP-2025-09) logged→closed in ~3 days, approver jones.harrison, root_cause "FX rate table refresh ran 4 hours late on BD2," claims corrective JE posted but corrective_journal_entry_id=null; recon **BL-CBBC1C9D68DD** still overdue with −595.28 + −238.11 residual, zero audit trail; GL 227000 flat at $2,203,570.05 with $0/$0 in FP-2025-09; recurs at FP-2026-02 (BL-1D75DEF62AFE).
   - **Systemic-but-open items on signed-off recons (Part B):** BL-BF341D66FE59 (ns 120000, CERTIFIED while exc_3e121025060b4b awaiting_approval), BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 (acme 101000, ARCHIVED while exc_abd8e711f5f347 awaiting_approval), plus BL-72E451657112 (ns 101000, systemic in_progress).
   - **Recurring breakdown = the SAP→Oracle subledger feed / BD3 cutover control**, with the "corrective JE" remediation recorded as done but never booked and "update BD3 checklist" promised every period but never effected.

6. All user messages:
   - (Prior session, core request — paraphrased) The full A–G mandate quoted in section 1: pin down recurring vs one-off breakdowns, verify whether documented fixes were implemented, get real scope (periods/entities/where it lands), leave review notes on every open-exception recon and every overdue/never-finished recon with real disposition + recurring/one-off + systemic flags, call out repeat-offender accounts, write up workpapers, raise a SINGLE tracked issue (not one per cause) with a real owner, email Julia copy Devon, post a short summary in the audit channel.
   - System reminders: MCP server instructions (peeragentsmcp); skills; date context (June 15, to ignore); repeated TodoWrite nudges.
   - **Current message:** "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools… Your entire response must be plain text: an <analysis> block followed by a <summary> block." (Earlier session had a similar text-only interruption.)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **(D)** Write findings workpaper to **Records Vault** (`records_vault_upload_document` — kind likely `audit_evidence` or `reconciliation_support`; needs classification + retention_policy_code).
   - **(E)** Raise **ONE** Linear issue (recurring SAP→Oracle feed/BD3 cutover breakdown; remediation-recorded-but-never-booked) with a real owner — NOT one per cause.
   - **(F)** Email Julia (julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com) cc Devon (devon.beale@brookfieldcpas.com), from ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com.
   - **(G)** Post a short summary in audit Slack channel **C007 (audit-engagements)** via `slack_conversations_add_message`.

8. Current Work:
   I **completed Part (C)** — posted all **20 review notes** on the overdue reconciliations (all returned success). Each note used author=ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com, header "[OVERDUE / NEVER-FINISHED — R. Delgado control-review evidence, as of 2026-06-12]", stating days-open, what's unreconciled, never-submitted/empty-audit-trail status, and recurring-overdue vs one-off. The 20 posted notes (RN ID → recon → account/period → days open → disposition):
   - RN-86819339 → BL-154CAE8E937B (ns 102000 Cash-Payroll, FP-2025-12, 158d) RECURRING
   - RN-290D9BD6 → BL-165E8FCE2971 (ns 102000, FP-2026-02, 102d) RECURRING
   - RN-64415367 → BL-18FFAE0E20D0 (acme 117000 Allowance Doubtful, FP-2026-02, 99d) ONE-OFF
   - RN-686FD8C1 → BL-1D27E9BB5152 (ns 235000 Payroll Tax W/H, FP-2026-03, 72d) ONE-OFF
   - RN-E9A5FB4A → BL-1D75DEF62AFE (brookfield 227000, FP-2026-02, 103d) RECURRING
   - RN-A9563EF2 → BL-293ABED60D4D (ns 101000 Cash-Operating, FP-2025-10, 221d, residual 7.45) ONE-OFF
   - RN-4805194B → BL-3EB5B519316B (acme 121000 Deferred Commissions LT, FP-2025-12, 161d) ONE-OFF
   - RN-18F1E111 → BL-409A3095FF02 (brookfield 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maint, FP-2025-11, 193d) RECURRING
   - RN-8772FF4C → BL-43515365C411 (ns 120000 Client Cost Advances, FP-2025-07, 313d — OLDEST) one-off straggler
   - RN-1534F03B → BL-55151C28DEC1 (brookfield 132000, FP-2026-03, 73d) RECURRING
   - RN-EB9B7EAA → BL-6C4B634CDCB8 (brookfield 260000 Partner Distributions Payable, FP-2026-03, 72d, residual −2.28) ONE-OFF
   - RN-75F671E1 → BL-81AC25A9C1F7 (ns 219000 AP-Employee Reimb, FP-2025-11, 189d) RECURRING (worst, 3 periods)
   - RN-93EF167F → BL-84508F49343C (ns 219000, FP-2025-12, 161d) RECURRING
   - RN-75BDEABD → BL-91589384DD4C (ns 210000 AP-External Vendors, FP-2025-12, 158d, residual 6.56) RECURRING cross-entity
   - RN-2F8EA753 → BL-A3D7273F3F2F (ns 225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses, FP-2026-04, 39d — newest) ONE-OFF
   - RN-289D75B0 → BL-B62A9864AADD (brookfield 102000 Cash-Payroll, FP-2025-10, 223d, residual −8.85) recurring Cash-Payroll theme
   - RN-BD22216B → BL-BE53BACC82D5 (acme 210000 AP-External Vendors, FP-2025-09, 252d, residual 9.19) RECURRING cross-entity
   - RN-B7DE9C4D → BL-CBBC1C9D68DD (brookfield 227000, FP-2025-09, 253d — KEYSTONE, −595.28+−238.11 unbooked) RECURRING
   - RN-3F360A89 → BL-D058E274A0C2 (brookfield 133000 Prepaid CPE & Training, FP-2025-08, 282d — 2nd oldest) ONE-OFF
   - RN-4B440A69 → BL-D2E1A6D4DDF9 (ns 219000 AP-Employee Reimb, FP-2026-04, 41d, residual 4.45) RECURRING (3rd period)
   Recurring-overdue accounts to call out: **ns 219000 (3 periods)**, **ns 102000 + brookfield 102000 Cash-Payroll**, **brookfield 132000 (frozen $1,075,804.83 across 2 periods)**, **brookfield 227000 (2 periods)**, **210000 AP-External Vendors across acme + ns**.
   I then gathered owner/channel/team data and pulled the 14 closed exceptions (the approver evidence in section 5) to choose a real Linear owner. TodoWrite has items 1–4 completed, item 5 (workpaper) in_progress, items 6–7 pending.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Proceed to **Part (D): write the findings workpaper to Records Vault** via `records_vault_upload_document`, then **(E)** create the single Linear issue with a real owner, **(F)** email Julia cc Devon, **(G)** post to Slack C007. This is directly in line with the user's request: "Write it up for the workpapers, raise the recurring breakdown as a single tracked issue, not one per cause, with a real owner, send Julia the findings and copy Devon ahead of the review, and post a short summary in the audit channel." For the Linear owner, the leading candidate is **george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com** (most senior tenure / firm review lead, identified the keystone 227000 exception, in the review chain) or assignment to the **Accounting Operations** team — NOT ryan.delgado (the raiser). Note for the workpaper/email: of the 14 never-booked closures, I (ryan.delgado) approved 3 (exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c, exc_4e5838d006f64c, exc_d8fc13aa2cc742) — disclose factually. (Note: the current user message demands TEXT ONLY with no tool calls, so the next tool actions occur only after this summary turn.)

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/d4cd6e96-a7df-45ae-877d-82d972e369ea.jsonl

content.content: {"result":"[{\"id\": \"linear_7d4b3f1127cd\", \"name\": \"Tax & Compliance Delivery\", \"key\": \"TCD\", \"description\": \"External client tax, entity compliance, and filing readiness team overseeing tax document chase lists, annual report catch-up, filing calendar exceptions, and audit/PBC support. The team reviews due-date risk tied to vendor support items like VEN-004-799133, follows period-specific adjustments including JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0004 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0002, and escalates unresolved approval items such as exc_abd8e711f5f347 and exc_cb0a9a94a3084c when they affect filing readiness or advisory delivery.\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-04-14T09:20:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-31T15:35:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"name\": \"Client Accounting Operations\", \"key\": \"CAO\", \"description\": \"Partner-led outsourced accounting team handling daily close blocker triage, client bank feed exceptions, cash posting follow-up, AP workflow reviews, and bookkeeping remediation for external clients. The team regularly works through reconciliation items like BL-18FFAE0E20D0 on 117000 Allowance for Doubtful Accounts, investigates open exceptions such as exc_af7274fb658844 and exc_4d5d3582698946, and coordinates invoice processing across items including VEN-019-583136 and VEN-012-211225 during monthly close and onboarding cycles.\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-03-03T08:45:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-20T17:10:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_acctops\", \"name\": \"Accounting Operations\", \"key\": \"ACCT\", \"description\": \"Accounting services delivery, review chains, and portfolio execution\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-11-10T17:55:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-11-10T17:55:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_tax\", \"name\": \"Tax\", \"key\": \"TAX\", \"description\": \"Corporate tax review, filings, and tax-accounting coordination\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-10-19T07:43:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-10-19T07:43:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_audit\", \"name\": \"Audit\", \"key\": \"AUD\", \"description\": \"Audit support, workpapers, and engagement review\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2023-02-24T17:31:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2023-02-24T17:31:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"team_comp\", \"name\": \"Compliance\", \"key\": \"COMP\", \"description\": \"AML, privacy, and policy oversight\", \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2021-04-20T19:10:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2021-04-20T19:10:00Z\"}]"}

[{"email_id": "b4b29060b3ae4c5e86839c1e7b895480", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Multi-state annual report catch-up control review", "content": "Hi Peter,

I have the inventory open and have separated the overdue annual reports into status-only items and items with potential blockers. Most of the delinquent filings are administrative catch-up matters, but I am still confirming state login access and any required portal credentials with @anaya.patel.

content.text: Devon Beale confirmed (`devon.beale@brookfieldcpas.com`, Audit Manager). Julia Vance and George McAdam confirmed as Linear users. Now I'll draft the findings workpaper and upload it to Records Vault.

input.content:
WORKPAPER — CONTROL REVIEW EVIDENCE
Recurring reconciliation breakdowns: "corrective action" recorded but never booked
====================================================================================

Prepared by:   Ryan Delgado, Audit Senior (ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com)
Date:          2026-06-12
For:           Julia Vance (control review); cc Devon Beale (Audit Manager)
Scope:         BlackLine close exceptions + reconciliations across entities
               brookfield / northstar_legal / acme_cloud
Sources relied on: BlackLine (exceptions, reconciliations, review notes),
               Oracle GL (account balances, journal entries), SAP Subledger
               (feed runs). BlackLine status fields were NOT relied on as
               evidence of disposition — they were corroborated to GL.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. THESIS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Reconciliation exception write-ups repeatedly promise the same corrective action on
closure ("Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh"), but
the corrective journal entry is never actually booked in Oracle GL. Because the fix
is recorded as done but never effected, the same breakdown re-surfaces period after
period in the same accounts and entities. The promised process fix ("Update BD3
cutover checklist to include this feed") is likewise recorded every time and never
implemented.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PRIMARY-SOURCE EVIDENCE — CLOSED EXCEPTIONS (Part A)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Population: all 14 CLOSED exceptions in BlackLine (state=closed).

Finding 2.1 — Every closed exception claims a corrective JE that does not exist.
  - All 14 closed exceptions carry corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL.
  - All 14 carry the IDENTICAL boilerplate resolution_summary:
      "Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh."
  - All 14 carry the IDENTICAL boilerplate lessons_learned:
      "Update BD3 cutover checklist to include this feed; flag in next-period
       control test."
  - All 14 are escalated=true, sox_implications=true, urgency=n_a.
  This is not a one-off documentation lapse: the corrective-JE field is empty in
  100% of closed cases while the narrative asserts the JE was posted.

Finding 2.2 — Six of the fourteen are flagged systemic_issue_flag=true:
      exc_143730e61ce742  brookfield 227000
      exc_151b0bee7e374e  brookfield 110000
      exc_3e229b67ef6147  brookfield 103000
      exc_5221568d4e4047  brookfield 235000
      exc_91530732a38046  brookfield 133000
      exc_d8fc13aa2cc742  brookfield 102000
  i.e. the firm's own records already classify the breakdown as systemic, yet the
  remediation pattern (record-but-don't-book) was applied to them anyway.

Finding 2.3 — Approvers of the never-booked closures (accountability trail):
      james.randall (x4), ryan.delgado (x3 — self, disclosed below),
      hannah.grant (x2), jones.harrison, alex.cahoon, daniel.jones,
      edith.banda, george.mcadam.
  DISCLOSURE: I (Ryan Delgado) approved 3 of these closures
      (exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c, exc_4e5838d006f64c, exc_d8fc13aa2cc742)
  relying on the boilerplate resolution narrative. They are included in the
  population without exception; this finding implicates the control, not any single
  approver, and I have not excluded my own approvals.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. KEYSTONE CASE — GL-VERIFIED (recurring, not one-off)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Exception exc_143730e61ce742 — brookfield account 227000, period FP-2025-09.
  - Logged -> closed in ~3 days; approver jones.harrison.
  - Root cause recorded: "FX rate table refresh ran 4 hours late on BD2."
  - Resolution claims corrective JE posted; corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL.
  - Oracle GL verification: account 227000 balance is FLAT at $2,203,570.05;
    FP-2025-09 shows $0 period debits and $0 period credits — NO corrective JE
    was ever booked.
  - The related reconciliation BL-CBBC1C9D68DD remains OVERDUE (253 days open as of
    2026-06-12) carrying unreconciled residual of -595.28 and -238.11, with no
    submission and an empty audit trail.
  - The SAME breakdown recurs at FP-2026-02 on the same account: reconciliation
    BL-1D75DEF62AFE (103 days overdue). This is the definition of recurring: same
    account, same root cause, two periods, fix never booked.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. RECURRING vs ONE-OFF — SCOPE (where it keeps landing)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The breakdown is concentrated in the SAP -> Oracle subledger feed / BD3 cutover
control, and it lands repeatedly in these accounts/entities:

RECURRING (repeat-offender accounts — overdue period after period):
  - northstar_legal 219000 (AP - Employee Reimbursements): 3 periods
        FP-2025-11 (BL-81AC25A9C1F7, 189d), FP-2025-12 (BL-84508F49343C, 161d),
        FP-2026-04 (BL-D2E1A6D4DDF9, 41d). WORST repeat offender.
  - Cash - Payroll (account 102000) across both northstar_legal and brookfield:
        ns FP-2025-12 (BL-154CAE8E937B, 158d), ns FP-2026-02 (BL-165E8FCE2971, 102d),
        brookfield FP-2025-10 (BL-B62A9864AADD, 223d, residual -8.85).
  - brookfield 132000 (Prepaid Facilities Maintenance): 2 periods, balance frozen
        $1,075,804.83 — FP-2025-11 (BL-409A3095FF02, 193d), FP-2026-03
        (BL-55151C28DEC1, 73d).
  - brookfield 227000: 2 periods (keystone above) — FP-2025-09 + FP-2026-02.
  - 210000 (AP - External Vendors) cross-entity: acme_cloud FP-2025-09
        (BL-BE53BACC82D5, 252d, residual 9.19) + northstar_legal FP-2025-12
        (BL-91589384DD4C, 158d, residual 6.56).

TRUE ONE-OFFS (isolated; no repeat in other periods observed):
  - acme_cloud 117000 Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (BL-18FFAE0E20D0)
  - northstar_legal 235000 Payroll Tax W/H (BL-1D27E9BB5152)
  - northstar_legal 101000 Cash-Operating FP-2025-10 (BL-293ABED60D4D, residual 7.45)
  - acme_cloud 121000 Deferred Commissions LT (BL-3EB5B519316B)
  - northstar_legal 120000 Client Cost Advances FP-2025-07 (BL-43515365C411, 313d —
        OLDEST open item; straggler)
  - brookfield 260000 Partner Distributions Payable (BL-6C4B634CDCB8, residual -2.28)
  - northstar_legal 225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses (BL-A3D7273F3F2F, 39d — newest)
  - brookfield 133000 Prepaid CPE & Training FP-2025-08 (BL-D058E274A0C2, 282d —
        2nd oldest)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. SYSTEMIC-BUT-STILL-OPEN (Part B — signed-off recons with live exceptions)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
These reconciliations were advanced through sign-off WHILE a related exception was
still unresolved — the status cannot be trusted as evidence of disposition:
  - BL-BF341D66FE59 (northstar_legal 120000): CERTIFIED while exc_3e121025060b4b is
        still awaiting_approval.
  - BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 (acme_cloud 101000): ARCHIVED while exc_abd8e711f5f347 is still
        awaiting_approval.
  - BL-72E451657112 (northstar_legal 101000): systemic, still in_progress.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. OPEN-ITEM + OVERDUE REVIEW NOTES POSTED (Parts B & C)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
For evidence, review notes were posted in BlackLine on EACH open-exception
reconciliation and EACH overdue/never-finished reconciliation, capturing the real
disposition (booked vs accepted timing difference; which period; who made it / who
it is waiting on), recurring-vs-one-off, and any systemic flag. 20 overdue/
never-finished reconciliations received notes (Part C); oldest is BL-43515365C411
at 313 days. Repeat-offender accounts singled out: ns 219000 (3 periods), Cash-
Payroll 102000 (ns + brookfield), brookfield 132000, brookfield 227000, 210000
AP-External Vendors (acme + ns).

NOTE on timestamps: review-note created_at is system-stamped 2026-06-15 with a
2026-06-17 SLA (platform clock). The analysis as-of date is 2026-06-12; day-counts
in the notes are measured to 2026-06-12.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. CONCLUSION / RECOMMENDATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The recurring breakdown is a single control failure, not a series of unrelated
incidents: corrective JEs are documented as booked but never posted to Oracle GL,
and the BD3 cutover checklist remediation is promised every period and never
implemented. Recommend:
  (a) Re-open / re-perform the affected reconciliations and BOOK the actual
      corrective JEs (or formally accept and document timing differences).
  (b) Replace the boilerplate resolution_summary / lessons_learned with
      evidence-linked closures (require a real corrective_journal_entry_id before
      an exception may close).
  (c) Actually implement the BD3 cutover checklist change for the SAP -> Oracle feed.
  (d) Track remediation under one owner (see linked Linear issue) — not one ticket
      per cause.

-- End of workpaper --

input.title: Systemic close-control failure: corrective JEs recorded as booked but never posted (SAP→Oracle feed / BD3 cutover)
input.description:
**Owner:** George McAdam (firm review lead) — single tracked remediation owner for this control failure. Do NOT split into one ticket per cause; this is one control.

**Raised by:** Ryan Delgado (Audit Senior), 2026-06-12, ahead of Julia Vance's control review (cc Devon Beale).
**Workpaper (evidence):** Records Vault doc_ba493ec24744 — "Control Review Evidence — Recurring reconciliation breakdowns" (classification confidential, AICPA_SQMS_7Y).

## What's broken (one control, not many incidents)
Reconciliation exceptions are closed with identical boilerplate ("Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh") but the corrective journal entry is **never actually booked in Oracle GL**. The promised process fix ("Update BD3 cutover checklist to include this feed") is recorded every time and **never implemented**. Because the fix is recorded-but-not-effected, the same breakdown re-surfaces period after period in the same accounts/entities.

## Primary-source evidence
- **All 14 closed exceptions** carry `corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL` despite the resolution narrative asserting a JE was posted. All are escalated=true, sox_implications=true.
- **6 of 14 are firm-flagged systemic** (227000, 110000, 103000, 235000, 133000, 102000 — all brookfield).
- **Keystone, GL-verified:** exc_143730e61ce742 (brookfield 227000, FP-2025-09) — GL 227000 flat at $2,203,570.05 with $0/$0 activity in FP-2025-09; no JE ever booked. Recurs at FP-2026-02. Recon BL-CBBC1C9D68DD overdue 253d with unbooked residual −595.28 / −238.11.

## Scope (recurring repeat-offender accounts)
- northstar_legal **219000** AP-Employee Reimb — **3 periods** (FP-2025-11/12, FP-2026-04). Worst.
- **102000** Cash-Payroll — ns (FP-2025-12, FP-2026-02) + brookfield (FP-2025-10).
- brookfield **132000** Prepaid Facilities Maint — 2 periods, balance frozen $1,075,804.83.
- brookfield **227000** — 2 periods (keystone).
- **210000** AP-External Vendors — cross-entity acme + ns.

## Signed-off but still open (status untrustworthy)
- BL-BF341D66FE59 (ns 120000) CERTIFIED while exc_3e121025060b4b awaiting_approval.
- BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 (acme 101000) ARCHIVED while exc_abd8e711f5f347 awaiting_approval.
- BL-72E451657112 (ns 101000) systemic, in_progress.

## Disclosure
Of the 14 never-booked closures, the raiser (Ryan Delgado) approved 3 (exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c, exc_4e5838d006f64c, exc_d8fc13aa2cc742) relying on the boilerplate. Included in population without exception — this implicates the control, not any single approver.

## Remediation (track here, one owner)
1. Re-open/re-perform affected recons and **book the actual corrective JEs** (or formally accept + document timing differences).
2. **Gate exception closure on a real `corrective_journal_entry_id`** — no boilerplate-only closes.
3. **Actually implement the BD3 cutover checklist change** for the SAP→Oracle feed.
4. Clear the overdue backlog (20 reconciliations carry review notes; oldest BL-43515365C411 at 313d).

Evidence: review notes posted on all open-exception and overdue reconciliations in BlackLine.

content.text: Linear issue **AUD-3117** created and assigned to George McAdam. Now Parts F & G — email Julia (cc Devon) and post to the audit Slack channel, in parallel.

input.subject: Control review findings: corrective JEs recorded but never booked (recurring close breakdown)
input.content:
Julia (cc Devon),

Ahead of the control review, here is where things stand on the reconciliation exceptions. Short version: the write-ups keep promising the same corrective action on closure, but the fix is recorded as done and never actually booked — so the same breakdowns keep getting re-logged. I went to Oracle GL and SAP directly rather than trusting the BlackLine status fields.

THE PATTERN (primary-source verified)
- All 14 CLOSED exceptions carry corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL, yet every one closes with the identical line "Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh" and the identical lessons-learned "Update BD3 cutover checklist to include this feed." All 14 are escalated, SOX-flagged. The corrective JE field is empty in 100% of closed cases.
- 6 of the 14 are flagged systemic by the firm's own records (brookfield 227000, 110000, 103000, 235000, 133000, 102000) — and the record-but-don't-book pattern was applied to them anyway.

KEYSTONE, GL-VERIFIED
exc_143730e61ce742 (brookfield 227000, FP-2025-09): closed in ~3 days claiming a corrective JE; Oracle GL shows account 227000 flat at $2,203,570.05 with $0/$0 activity in FP-2025-09 — no JE was ever booked. The recon (BL-CBBC1C9D68DD) is still overdue at 253 days with unbooked residual of -595.28 / -238.11, and the same breakdown recurs at FP-2026-02 on the same account.

SCOPE — where it keeps landing (recurring, period after period)
- northstar_legal 219000 (AP-Employee Reimb): 3 periods — worst repeat offender.
- 102000 Cash-Payroll: northstar_legal (2 periods) + brookfield.
- brookfield 132000 (Prepaid Facilities Maint): 2 periods, balance frozen at $1,075,804.83.
- brookfield 227000: 2 periods (keystone).
- 210000 AP-External Vendors: cross-entity (acme_cloud + northstar_legal).
The rest are true one-offs (e.g., acme 117000 allowance, brookfield 260000 partner distributions, the FP-2025-07 ns 120000 straggler at 313 days).

SYSTEMIC BUT STILL UNRESOLVED (status can't be trusted)
- BL-BF341D66FE59 (ns 120000) was CERTIFIED while exc_3e121025060b4b is still awaiting approval.
- BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 (acme 101000) was ARCHIVED while exc_abd8e711f5f347 is still awaiting approval.
- BL-72E451657112 (ns 101000) is systemic and still in progress.

WHAT I'VE DONE
- Left review notes in BlackLine on every open-exception reconciliation and every overdue/never-finished reconciliation (20 overdue items, oldest 313 days), each capturing the real disposition, recurring vs one-off, and systemic flag.
- Filed the full workpaper to Records Vault (doc_ba493ec24744, confidential, 7-yr retention).
- Raised ONE tracked issue, not one per cause: Linear AUD-3117, owner George McAdam, for the remediation (book the actual JEs, gate closures on a real JE id, implement the BD3 checklist change, clear the backlog).

Disclosure: of the 14 never-booked closures, I approved 3 (exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c, exc_4e5838d006f64c, exc_d8fc13aa2cc742) relying on the boilerplate. I've included them in the population without exception — this is a control problem, not one approver's.

Happy to walk through any of it before the review.

Ryan
Ryan Delgado, Audit Senior

input.payload:
*Control-review heads-up — recurring reconciliation breakdown (evidence filed)*

Pattern, verified against Oracle GL/SAP (not BlackLine status): exception write-ups close with the same boilerplate "Corrective JE posted…" but the JE is never actually booked, so the same breakdowns keep re-logging.

• All 14 closed exceptions have `corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL`; 6 are firm-flagged systemic.
• Keystone GL-verified: brookfield 227000 (FP-2025-09) — GL flat, $0/$0 activity, no JE ever booked; recon still overdue 253d, recurs FP-2026-02.
• Repeat offenders: ns 219000 (3 periods), 102000 Cash-Payroll (ns+brookfield), brookfield 132000 ($1.08M frozen), 227000, 210000 AP-External (acme+ns).
• Signed-off-but-open: BL-BF341D66FE59 certified & BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 archived while their exceptions are still awaiting approval.

Done: review notes on all open + 20 overdue recons (oldest 313d); workpaper in Records Vault (doc_ba493ec24744); single tracked issue *AUD-3117* (owner: George McAdam) — not one per cause. Findings emailed to Julia, cc Devon, ahead of the review. — Ryan

content.text:
All seven parts are complete. Here's where things stand.

## What I found (verified against Oracle GL / SAP, not BlackLine status)

**The pattern is real and systemic.** Exception write-ups close with identical boilerplate — *"Corrective JE posted; variance cleared in subsequent recon refresh"* — but the journal entry is never actually booked, so the same breakdowns keep getting re-logged.

- **All 14 closed exceptions** carry `corrective_journal_entry_id = NULL` while the narrative claims a JE was posted. Same boilerplate "fix" (update the BD3 cutover checklist) promised every time, never implemented. **6 of 14 are firm-flagged systemic.**
- **Keystone, GL-proven:** brookfield 227000 (FP-2025-09) — GL flat at $2,203,570.05 with $0/$0 activity in the period; no JE ever booked. Recon BL-CBBC1C9D68DD still overdue 253 days with unbooked −595.28/−238.11 residual, and it **recurs** at FP-2026-02.
- **Repeat-offender accounts:** ns 219000 (3 periods, worst), 102000 Cash-Payroll (ns + brookfield), brookfield 132000 ($1.08M frozen across 2 periods), brookfield 227000, and 210000 AP-External Vendors cross-entity (acme + ns). The rest are true one-offs.
- **Status can't be trusted:** BL-BF341D66FE59 was *certified* and BL-5A8EA3B0B7B2 was *archived* while their exceptions are still awaiting approval.

## Actions taken (documenting, not fixing)
- **Review notes** posted on every open-exception reconciliation and all **20 overdue/never-finished** recons (oldest 313 days), each with real disposition, recurring vs one-off, and systemic flag.
- **Workpaper** filed to Records Vault — `doc_ba493ec24744` (confidential, 7-yr AICPA retention).
- **One** tracked Linear issue — **AUD-3117**, owner **George McAdam** (not one per cause) — with the remediation steps (book the real JEs, gate closures on an actual JE id, implement the BD3 checklist change, clear the backlog).
- **Email** sent to Julia, cc Devon, ahead of the review (`email_id 3db03812…`).
- **Slack** summary posted to #audit-engagements (C007).

I disclosed in both the workpaper and the email that of the 14 never-booked closures, I approved 3 myself relying on the boilerplate — included in the population without exception, since this is a control failure rather than any one approver's.

One note for transparency: the BlackLine review notes and the Records Vault upload were system-stamped 2026-06-15 (platform clock); all day-counts and the as-of date in the content are measured to 2026-06-12 as intended.
